title: The Wind Waker HD
lede: The whole adventure on one page. Open a section as you reach it — and flip on Completionist mode to layer in upgrade timing, easy Pieces of Heart, and 100% checklists.

sections.title: Outset Island
steps: Talk to Aryll for your telescope, then watch a giant bird drop a girl into the forest.
Visit Orca below for a sword lesson, head up the forest path, and rescue Tetra.
The bird snatches Aryll instead — board Tetra's pirate ship to give chase.

advisories.title: Bank Orca's 500-hit reward early
advisories.body: Land 500 cumulative sword hits on Orca across visits for a Piece of Heart. Chip away now while it's free — it doubles as combat practice you'll want anyway.

advisories.title: Telescope the bird
advisories.body: Watching through the telescope triggers the cutscene that starts the game. If nothing happens, you haven't zoomed in on it yet.

collectibles.label: Piece of Heart — Orca (500 hits)
collectibles.note: Spar with Orca over multiple visits. Easiest very-early heart in the game.

collectibles.label: Piece of Heart — Outset cliff cave
collectibles.note: Reachable later once you can return with the right tools. Note it now.

sections.title: The Forsaken Fortress
steps: You're flung in weaponless. Grab a barrel and wear it — guards ignore a barrel that holds still when searchlights sweep.
Move only in the dark gaps between rotating lights. Caught = back to the start cell.
Free the captured girls, reach Aryll up top — then the giant bird hurls you out to sea and the talking boat, the King of Red Lions, rescues you.

advisories.title: There is no sword to find here
advisories.body: Don't waste time hunting for a weapon — the whole chapter is built around avoidance, not combat. Patience beats speed.

advisories.title: This is a 'watch it' section
advisories.body: Searchlight timing is much easier to copy than to read. If you're getting caught, the embedded video shows the exact safe route.

sections.title: Dragon Roost — Din's Pearl
steps: Deliver the letter, help Prince Komali find his courage, and grab the Grappling Hook inside the cavern.
Use bomb-flowers and the hook to climb the lava chambers to Valoo's tail.
Boss — Gohma: grapple the ceiling support to drop rock on its head, then strike the eye. Repeat ×3.

advisories.title: Photograph Gohma if you have the Deluxe Picto Box
advisories.body: If you've already picked up the camera, snap Gohma here for the Nintendo Gallery. It's far more convenient than the late-game alternative. No camera yet? Skip it — you can return.

advisories.title: The Grappling Hook also steals
advisories.body: It pulls rupees and items off enemies all game. Worth remembering when you're short on cash for chart deciphering later.

collectibles.label: Piece of Heart — Dragon Roost paths
collectibles.note: A couple are tucked around the island's cliffs and the postman's route.

sections.title: Forbidden Woods — Farore's Pearl
steps: Pick up the Boomerang — this dungeon's key item, which locks onto multiple targets at once and drives most of the puzzles.
Ride Deku flowers across gaps to reach high platforms and escort the seedling sage Makar.
Boss — Kalle Demos: boomerang the six vines holding it up, then slash the exposed bulb.

advisories.title: Grab the mailbox Piece of Heart on the way out
advisories.body: After the dungeon, the King of Red Lions' postbox is shaking — a letter delivers a free Piece of Heart. Easy to sail straight past forever.

collectibles.label: Piece of Heart — Forest Haven letter
collectibles.note: Check the shaking postbox after the dungeon.

sections.title: Greatfish, Jabun & Nayru's Pearl
steps: The story turns hard: Greatfish Isle is destroyed and Ganondorf is now hunting you directly.
Return to the pirates, gain Bombs, and blast open Jabun's cave behind Outset for Nayru's Pearl.
Place all three Pearls on their statues to raise the Tower of the Gods from the sea.

advisories.title: This is the upgrade window — you can finally sail freely AND you have Bombs
advisories.body: Several permanent upgrades open up the moment you have bombs and a cannon. Detouring now makes the entire back half of the game easier. Flip on Completionist mode for the exact list.

advisories.title: Great Fairy upgrades (spread across the map's corners)
advisories.body: The corner Great Fairies permanently upgrade your Bomb Bag (30→60→99) and your Wallet (1,000→5,000 rupees). Some of their caves must be bombed open, which is why you couldn't reach them earlier. The Quiver upgrade needs the Bow — that arrives next chapter.

advisories.title: Why the bigger wallet matters
advisories.body: Late game you'll pay Tingle to decipher Triforce charts — that costs serious rupees. Grabbing the wallet upgrade now saves a painful grind later.

collectibles.label: Bomb Bag upgrade ×2
collectibles.note: From Great Fairies once you can bomb their caves open. Capacity 30→60→99.

collectibles.label: Wallet upgrade (→5,000 rupees)
collectibles.note: Critical for Tingle's chart-deciphering fees endgame.

collectibles.label: Easy Pieces of Heart while sailing
collectibles.note: Lookout platforms, submarines, and Windfall minigames are the lowest-effort hearts. Bolster your health before the temples.

sections.title: Tower of the Gods
steps: Collect the Bow — the dungeon's key item. Many rooms use a statue you command with the Command Melody.
Boss — Gohdan: arrow both hands to stun it, arrow the nose, then drop a bomb in its mouth to end each phase.

advisories.title: Now you have the Bow — go claim the Quiver upgrades
advisories.body: With the Bow in hand, the remaining Great Fairies will upgrade your Quiver (arrows 30→60→99). Worth a short sailing detour before the endgame ramps up.

collectibles.label: Quiver upgrade ×2
collectibles.note: From Great Fairies, now reachable with bombs + the Bow.

sections.title: Hyrule & the Master Sword
steps: The sea hides drowned Hyrule. You draw the Master Sword — and Tetra is revealed as Princess Zelda.
Ganondorf attacks; you must return to the Forsaken Fortress to rescue Zelda and Aryll.

advisories.title: Your Master Sword starts powerless
advisories.body: Restoring its power — by awakening two new sages — is the spine of the back half of the game. Don't expect it to one-shot anything yet.

advisories.title: Stock up before you commit
advisories.body: Top off arrows, bombs, potions and Fairies in bottles now. The next stretch is long and you won't want to sail back mid-temple.

sections.title: Forsaken Fortress II, the Temples & Ganon
steps: Return to the Fortress and beat the Helmaroc King (Skull Hammer cracks the mask).
Awaken the sages Medli (Earth Temple) and Makar (Wind Temple) to restore the Master Sword.
Gather the eight Triforce of Courage shards using deciphered charts, then storm Ganon's Tower for the finale: parry + Light Arrows, ending with the sword to the forehead.

advisories.title: Decipher charts in bulk with the rupees you saved
advisories.body: Now is when the early wallet upgrade pays off — Tingle's fees for the Triforce charts add up fast. Batch all eight charts before you start salvaging so you're not sailing back and forth.

advisories.title: Last call for the Nintendo Gallery
advisories.body: If you're chasing 100%, photograph any remaining figures with the Deluxe Picto Box before the point of no return — several enemies and bosses don't reappear.

collectibles.label: 8 × Triforce of Courage shards
collectibles.note: Charts → Tingle deciphers → salvage from the sea floor.

collectibles.label: Nintendo Gallery figurines
collectibles.note: The true completionist grind — every character and enemy photographed.
